* Arthur JC (1924) The Uredinales (Rusts) of Iowa. Proceedings of the Iowa Academy of Science 31(1), 229-255.
------- as Malus malus

* Zhao P, Qi XH, Crous PW, Duan WJ, Cai L (2020) Gymnosporangium species on Malus: species delineation, diversity and host alternation. Persoonia 45, 68-100.
------- from the molecular and morphological study of herbarium specimens. As Malus domestica and Malus pumila.

* Neely D, Himelick EB (1961) Additional juniper hosts of cedar-apple and cedar-hawthorn rusts. Plant Disease Reporter 45, 351.
------- slight infestation. from observations in the juniperus collection of an arboretum in Illinois.

* Neely D, Himelick EB (1961) Additional juniper hosts of cedar-apple and cedar-hawthorn rusts. Plant Disease Reporter 45, 351.
------- slight infestation, as Juniperus horizontalis f. plumosa. From observations in the juniperus collection of an arboretum in Illinois.

Toggle navigation Gymnosporangium juniperi-virginianae (GYMNJV) Menu Overview → Distribution Host plants Host commodities Categorization Reporting Photos Documents EPPO links Overview Code created in: 2002-03-11 Basic information EPPO Code: GYMNJV Preferred name: Gymnosporangium juniperi-virginianae Authority: Schweinitz Other scientific names Name Authority Aecidium pyrolatum Schweinitz Gymnosporangium macropus Link Gymnosporangium virginianum Sprengel Roestelia pyrata Thaxter Common names Name Language American rust of apple English cedar/apple rust English rust of American cedar English rust of juniper English rouille de Virginie du pommier French rouille du genévrier French roya del enebro Spanish roya de Virginia del manzano Spanish ржавчиа кедра яблока Russian more photos... Taxonomy Kingdom Fungi      (
      1FUNGK      ) Phylum Basidiomycota      (
      1BASIP      ) Class Pucciniomycotina      (
      1TELIC      ) Subclass Pucciniomycetes      (
      1PUCCL      ) Order Pucciniales      (
      1UREDO      ) Family Gymnosporangiaceae      (
      1GYMNF      ) Genus Gymnosporangium      (
      1GYMNG      ) Species Gymnosporangium juniperi-virginianae      (
      GYMNJV      )

seed.symptoms: Bright orange-yellow spots on upper leaf surface; tube-like structures (aecia) on lower surface; distorted fruit.
seed.causes: Fungus Gymnosporangium juniperi-virginianae; requires two hosts (apple/crabapple AND Eastern red cedar/juniper) to complete life cycle.
seed.treatment: Apply myclobutanil or triadimefon fungicides during primary infection period (pink bud through petal fall).
seed.prevention: Remove nearby juniper/red cedar hosts; plant rust-resistant apple cultivars.
